name: Las Torretas from Navalvillar de Pela – round trip via Sierra de Pela
name_original: Las Torretas desde Navalvillar de Pela – ida y vuelta por Sierra de Pela
region_intro: Hiking trails around Navalvillar De Pela traverse a varied landscape in the Extremadura region of Spain, characterized by the Sierra de Pela mountain range with its Mediterranean forests of holm and cork oaks. The area is also defined by the Embalse de Orellana, a significant reservoir and wetland, and the meandering Guadiana River. These features contribute to a diverse environment for outdoor exploration, including entry points to the La Siberia Biosphere Reserve. Best hiking trails around Navalvillar De Pela The most popular hiking route is Orellana la Vieja from Playa de las Casetas – circular by the Embalse and Canal de Orellana, a 10.2 miles (16.5 km) trail that takes 4 hours 26 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the Orellana Reservoir and Canal. Another top favourite among local hikers is Las Torretas from Navalvillar de Pela – round trip via Sierra de Pela, a moderate 5.5 miles (8.9 km) path. This route leads through the Sierra de Pela, offering views of the surrounding natural environment. Local hikers also love the Puente Cogolludo from Orellana de la Sierra – round trip through the Parque del Embalse de Orellana and Sierra de Pela, a 12.0 miles (19.3 km) trail leading through the Parque del Embalse de Orellana and Sierra de Pela, often completed in about 5 hours 15 minutes. Hiking around Navalvillar De Pela is defined by Mediterranean forests, extensive reservoirs, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy reservoir trails to more challenging routes through the Sierra de Pela. The routes in Navalvillar De Pela are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.7 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 20 hikers have used komoot to explore Navalvillar De Pela's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking around Navalvillar De Pela?
A: Hiking around Navalvillar De Pela offers a diverse range of landscapes. You'll encounter the dense Mediterranean forests of the Sierra de Pela, characterized by holm and cork oaks, which is also a Special Protection Area for Birds. The area is also defined by the vast Embalse de Orellana reservoir and the meandering Guadiana River, creating wetlands that support a rich variety of birdlife. You can also find entry points to the ecologically significant La Siberia Biosphere Reserve.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Navalvillar De Pela?
A: Yes, there are several easy trails. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990700585/sierra-brava-reservoir-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Brava Reservoir Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.3 km route around the Sierra Brava Reservoir, suitable for all levels. The Vía Verde Vegas del Guadiana also offers an accessible path, following a former railway line, which is excellent for walking and birdwatching.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Navalvillar De Pela?
A: The best time to enjoy outdoor activities in Navalvillar De Pela is generally during spring and autumn. During these seasons, temperatures are milder, making hiking and cycling more comfortable. Spring also offers excellent opportunities for observing aquatic birds around the Embalse de Orellana.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, you can find circular routes. A notable one is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1101892674/orellana-la-vieja-from-playa-de-las-casetas-circular-by-the-embalse-and-canal-de-orellana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orellana la Vieja from Playa de las Casetas – circular by the Embalse and Canal de Orellana&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 16.5 km trail that follows the Orellana Reservoir and Canal.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see on the trails?
A: Navalvillar De Pela is rich in wildlife, especially birds. The Sierra de Pela is a Special Protection Area for Birds (ZEPA), where you might spot griffon vultures and golden eagles. The Embalse de Orellana and the Guadiana River wetlands are home to aquatic birds like storks, egrets, and ibises. The Dehesa de Moheda Alta is particularly famous for crane sightings during autumn and winter.

Q: Are there any challenging hiking trails for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for more experienced hikers, there are challenging options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1266935539/guadiana-natural-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiana Natural Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a longer, more difficult route of nearly 27 km, offering diverse terrain within the La Siberia Biosphere Reserve. Another difficult option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1101829074/puente-cogolludo-from-orellana-de-la-sierra-round-trip-through-the-parque-del-embalse-de-orellana-and-sierra-de-pela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Cogolludo from Orellana de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, a 19.3 km trail through the Parque del Embalse de Orellana and Sierra de Pela.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or points of interest to explore along the hiking routes?
A: Along the hiking routes, you can explore various points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1813326" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde Vegas del Guadiana&lt;/a&gt; offers birdwatching viewpoints. You might also encounter &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1757547" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;stork nests&lt;/a&gt;. For historical interest, hikers can explore the ruins of the Ancient Roman City of Lacimurga in the Cogolludo area. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5673577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Pedro Apóstol in Casas de Don Pedro&lt;/a&gt; is another notable landmark nearby.

Q: How long do hikes typically take in Navalvillar De Pela?
A: Hike durations vary depending on the trail's length and difficulty. Easy routes like the Sierra Brava Reservoir Trail can take around 1 hour 10 minutes. Moderate trails, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1102968568/las-torretas-from-navalvillar-de-pela-round-trip-via-sierra-de-pela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Torretas from Navalvillar de Pela&lt;/a&gt;, typically take around 2 hours 37 minutes. Longer, more difficult routes can extend to 5 hours or more, like the Puente Cogolludo trail which takes about 5 hours 15 minutes.

Q: Are there any specific areas known for good viewpoints?
A: Yes, the Sierra de Pela offers views of the surrounding natural environment, and the Embalse de Orellana provides expansive water views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1813308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Birdwatching viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed for scenic observation. The Camino de las Tres Cruces, within La Siberia Extremeña, is also known for its panoramic views.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Navalvillar De Pela?
A: The trails in Navalvillar De Pela are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.7 stars from over 6 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, from Mediterranean forests to extensive reservoirs, and the options available for different ability levels.

Q: Can I access hiking trails using public transport in Navalvillar De Pela?
A: While specific public transport links directly to trailheads are limited, the Vía Verde Vegas del Guadiana, an accessible path, is a good option for those looking to explore without a car. For other trails, it's generally recommended to have private transport to reach the starting points, especially for routes deeper into the Sierra de Pela or around the reservoirs.
tips: The hiking route starts from the town of Navalvillar de Pela on the southwest side towards the Sierra de Pela. The Camino de las Tres Cruces is not difficult and follows a gravel path.
Mirador de Las Torretas is located near the Cerro de las Tres Cruces peak and the town of Navalvillar de Pela

Navalvillar de Pela is the gateway to the region of La Siberia on the route that comes from Villanueva de la Serena. In ancient times it belonged to Trujillo, in Cáceres, and its inhabitants are known as 'peleños'.

waypoints.name: View of Navalvillar de Pela from Mirador de Las Torretas
